message:
feat: add ClawdBot provider with simple JSONL message format

Port ClawdBot connector from CASS — simplest provider with bare
role/content/timestamp JSONL lines. Includes reader, writer,
detection, and 17 unit tests. Closes bd-1e2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-1a2.4","title":"Reverse-engineer Claude Code session directory naming","description":"Investigate and document how Claude Code derives the project directory key under ~/.claude/projects/ and how resume identity maps to files.

Research method:
1. Correlate existing ~/.claude/projects/* directories with known workspace paths.
2. Inspect Claude CLI/package behavior and source (if available).
3. Cross-check with CASS Claude connector assumptions and explicitly note matches/differences.
4. Validate with synthetic write + real Claude resume attempt.

Required outputs:
- Verified algorithm: workspace path -> project directory key.
- Verified session filename/ID behavior used by resume.
- Determination of whether additional manifests/index files are required for resume discovery.
- Rust helper implementation in casr with tests against multiple real samples.
- Documentation in src/providers/claude_code.rs and docs/cass-porting-notes.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Algorithm validated against at least 3 real workspace/project-dir pairs.
- Helper function reproduces all sampled keys deterministically.
- Resume ID/file mapping is documented with concrete examples.
- Any divergence from CASS assumptions is explicitly documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:43:50.559354910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:10:55.862303901Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:10:55.862285587Z","close_reason":"Reverse-engineered and implemented project_dir_key(): all non-alphanumeric chars → hyphens. Validated against real ~/.claude/projects/ directories. Tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["claude-code","research","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:08.023460308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:45.104491128Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1a2.5","title":"Reverse-engineer Codex session resume mechanism","description":"Investigate and document Codex resume resolution semantics: how `codex resume <id>` maps IDs to session files and which file fields are mandatory.

Research method:
1. Inspect Codex CLI help/behavior and source where available.
2. Probe ~/.codex/sessions structure across multiple real sessions.
3. Create controlled synthetic session files and test acceptance/rejection.
4. Compare findings against CASS Codex connector assumptions and document deltas.

Required outputs:
- Verified lookup algorithm (path-based, embedded ID-based, index-based, or hybrid).
- Minimum required JSONL/JSON fields for resume acceptance.
- Confirmation of any checksum/signature/index requirements.
- Concrete mapping examples from observed session IDs to file paths.
- Documentation in src/providers/codex.rs and docs/cass-porting-notes.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Resume lookup behavior is validated empirically with real/synthetic cases.
- Mandatory vs optional fields are clearly listed.
- Research findings directly inform writer implementation rules in bd-1a2.2.
- Any divergence from CASS assumptions is explicitly documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:03.752649488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:10:55.959813778Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:10:55.959787269Z","close_reason":"Reverse-engineered Codex session resume: ULID session IDs, rollout-TIMESTAMP-ID.jsonl naming, session_meta payload.id, date-hierarchy directories. rollout_path() helper + owns_session with 3-tier lookup (path, filename suffix, body). Tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codex","research","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:07.824387932Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:45.181214064Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1a2.6","title":"Reverse-engineer Gemini CLI session resume mechanism","description":"Investigate and document Gemini session discovery/resume semantics, including session ID mapping, hash directory derivation, and minimum acceptable file shape.

Research method:
1. Inspect Gemini CLI commands/help and source where available.
2. Examine ~/.gemini/tmp/*/chats layout and any related state/index files.
3. Create synthetic sessions and validate acceptance/rejection paths.
4. Compare findings with CASS Gemini connector assumptions and document deltas.

Required outputs:
- Verified resume/discovery mechanism (explicit resume command vs implicit discovery behavior).
- Verified relationship between sessionId, filename, and hash-directory placement.
- Minimum required JSON fields and any required sibling artifacts.
- Documentation in src/providers/gemini.rs and docs/cass-porting-notes.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- Gemini acceptance behavior is validated with reproducible experiments.
- Hash/ID/path rules are documented with concrete examples.
- Research findings directly inform writer implementation rules in bd-1a2.3.
- Any divergence from CASS assumptions is explicitly documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:14.313844502Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:10:56.055019514Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:10:56.054999035Z","close_reason":"Reverse-engineered Gemini session resume: SHA256(workspace) project hash, session-TIMESTAMP-UUIDprefix.json naming, sessionId in body. project_hash() and session_filename() helpers. Tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gemini","research","writers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:07.625103399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1a2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:45.258155228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1e2","title":"Implement ClawdBot provider adapter (simplest — direct JSONL)","description":"PORT ClawdBot provider from CASS: /dp/coding_agent_session_search/src/connectors/clawdbot.rs

WHAT IS CLAWDBOT: A Claude chatbot that stores sessions as simple JSONL files.

SESSION FORMAT:
  Location: ~/.clawdbot/sessions/*.jsonl
  Env override: CLAWDBOT_HOME
  Each line is a standalone JSON message: {"role":"user|assistant|system","content":"...","timestamp":"2025-01-27T03:30:00.000Z"}
  No wrapper, no content blocks, no session metadata header — just raw messages.

PROVIDER TRAIT IMPLEMENTATION:
  - slug: "clawdbot"
  - cli_alias: "cwb"
  - name: "ClawdBot"
  - detect(): Check for ~/.clawdbot/sessions/ directory (or CLAWDBOT_HOME)
  - session_roots(): Return [~/.clawdbot/sessions/]
  - owns_session(id): Look for {id}.jsonl in session roots
  - read_session(path): Parse JSONL lines into CanonicalMessages (role→normalize_role, content as-is, timestamp→parse_timestamp)
  - write_session(session, opts): Generate JSONL file with one {"role","content","timestamp"} per line. Use atomic_write.
  - resume_command(id): "clawdbot --resume {id}" (best guess — no documented resume mechanism)

CONSIDERATIONS:
  - Simplest provider to port — good first pick to validate the porting workflow
  - No workspace tracking (ChatGPT-like: set workspace=None)
  - Title: extract from first user message using truncate_title()
  - Session ID: derive from filename stem
  - Timestamps: ISO-8601 strings (parse_timestamp handles this)
  - No model tracking, no tool calls, no content blocks

REFERENCE: Follow Aider/ChatGPT provider pattern for structure. CASS connector is ~100 lines.

FILES TO CREATE/MODIFY:
  - CREATE: src/providers/clawdbot.rs
  - MODIFY: src/providers/mod.rs (add pub mod clawdbot)
  - MODIFY: src/discovery.rs (add to default_registry, add JSONL signature check)
  - MODIFY: src/main.rs (update provider list in docs)
  - MODIFY: tests/json_contract_test.rs (update provider count + alias map + CLAWDBOT_HOME env)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:05:12.049803222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:21.270063260Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

  - Session ID: derive from filename stem

 {"id":"bd-1fb","title":"Propagate provider slug into atomic_write/restore_backup SessionWriteError","description":"pipeline::atomic_write currently constructs CasrError::SessionWriteError with an empty provider string, which weakens actionable error messages. Thread provider slug through atomic_write (and restore_backup if needed) so write/rollback failures report the correct provider.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:49:41.310444594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:56:15.152022972Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T01:56:15.151996382Z","close_reason":"Threaded provider slug through pipeline::atomic_write and restore_backup; updated all provider call sites + atomic_write tests so SessionWriteError now reports the correct provider; cargo fmt/clippy/test pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["errors","pipeline"]}
 {"id":"bd-1kg","title":"EPIC: CLI Interface & UX","description":"Implement the casr CLI using clap.

Primary usage pattern: `casr <target-agent> resume <session-id>`.

Target agents:
- `cc` (Claude Code)
- `cod` (Codex)
- `gmi` (Gemini CLI)

Additional subcommands:
- `casr list` (list sessions across providers)
- `casr info <session-id>` (inspect a session without converting)
- `casr providers` (detection status + evidence)
- `casr completions <shell>`

The CLI must:
- auto-detect the source provider from the session ID (with explicit ambiguity errors)
- show clear progress + actionable failures
- report success with the exact resume command for the target provider
- support `--json` output for automation

Uses color with TTY detection; colors must be disabled automatically for `--json` and non-TTY output.

## Success Criteria

- Primary `resume` flow is fast, safe (atomic writes + backup), and self-verifying.
- `list/info/providers` are useful for discovery and debugging without conversion.
- All commands support `--json` with stable schemas and contract tests.
- Error messages include remediation hints (install, `--source`, `--force`, etc.).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:38:09.242303221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:16:04.594813342Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:16:04.594733863Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg","depends_on_id":"bd-3my","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:05.135990126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:05.056337066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1kg.1","title":"Implement CLI argument parsing with clap","description":"Implement the clap CLI in `src/main.rs`. Use clap derive macros.

## Commands

Usage patterns:

- `casr <target> resume <session-id>` (primary)
- `casr list [--provider <slug>] [--workspace <path>] [--limit <n>] [--sort <field>]`
- `casr info <session-id>`
- `casr providers`
- `casr completions <bash|zsh|fish>`
- `casr --version`
- `casr --help`

Where `<target>` is one of: `cc` (Claude Code), `cod` (Codex), `gmi` (Gemini CLI).

## Global Flags

- `--dry-run` : show what would happen without writing.
- `--force` : overwrite existing target session if it exists (creates backups).
- `--json` : machine-readable JSON output (no ANSI color, stable field names).
- `--verbose` : enable debug-level logs.
- `--trace` : enable trace-level logs.
- `--source <alias_or_path>` : pin source provider alias (`cc|cod|gmi`) OR a direct native session file path.
- `--enrich` : opt-in enrichment messages (bd-ikb.4).

Logging note:
- `--verbose`/`--trace` must configure the tracing subscriber filter directly (do not claim to "set RUST_LOG" at runtime).
- If `RUST_LOG` is already set, `--verbose`/`--trace` should override only for the `casr` target (documented precedence).

## Acceptance Criteria

- clap surface matches the README command reference.
- `--source` accepts either a known alias or a path; invalid values produce actionable usage errors.
- `--json` mode is supported across commands and disables color automatically.
- `--verbose` and `--trace` are wired to tracing subscriber filtering (bd-2zw.6).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:41:48.436160082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T12:56:17.619805733Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T12:56:17.619702089Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:08.421300610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1kg.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3my.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:50.554982969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
